senses.definition: producing or involving birefringence, i.e., causing a material to split a light beam into two rays with different directions
examples: A birefracting crystal splits light into two rays.
The crystal shows birefracting properties under polarized light.

senses.definition: the process of light being split into two rays when it passes through certain materials (double refraction)
examples: Birefraction occurs in calcite crystals.
The optical phenomenon of birefraction explains the double image.

senses.definition: relating to or exhibiting birefringence; having a property that splits light into two rays
examples: Birefractive materials behave differently for polarized light.
Calcite is a birefractive crystal.

senses.definition: the property of a material to split light into two rays that travel at different speeds and directions
examples: Birefringence explains why certain crystals show two colors.
The measured birefringence helps identify the mineral.

senses.definition: plural of birefringence; multiple instances of double refraction in different materials or directions
examples: Different samples show varying birefringences under polarized light.
Researchers compared the birefringences of several minerals.

senses.definition: not applicable
an ancient sailing ship with two rows of oars on each side
examples: The ship was an early example of oared galley transport.
Historians study the biremes in ancient Mediterranean trade.

senses.definition: plural of bireme; ancient ships with two rows of oars on each side
examples: Ancient fleets included biremes in naval warfare.
Artefacts depict several biremes moored at the harbor.

senses.definition: a square cap with three or four ridges worn in the Catholic Church and by some academics
examples: The professor wore a black biretta at the ceremony.
Students in the old university outfit carried their birettas.

senses.definition: plural of biretta; multiple square caps worn as part of formal dress in certain institutions
examples: The clergy from several parishes wore their birettas.
Historic costumes included birettas and robes.

senses.definition: unrecognized or obscure term in English; no stable senses found
a genus of terrestrial hermit crabs, large crustaceans
examples: Birgus latro is the coconut crab, the largest terrestrial arthropod.
Birgus species are found on tropical islands.

senses.definition: a Sri Lankan fermented beverage; or a shorthand for biriban in some contexts; very region-specific usage
examples: In some communities, biri is enjoyed as a festive drink.
The term biri may appear in regional menus or folklore.

senses.definition: alternate spelling of biryani, a spiced mixed rice dish with meat or vegetables
examples: We ordered biriyani for dinner.
Biryani is a popular dish in South Asian cuisine.

senses.definition: plural of biriyani; multiple servings of the spiced rice dish
examples: The table offered several birianis from different regions.
We shared two birianis among the family.

senses.definition: a tropical fruit (monstera deliciosa) or a ceremonial term in some languages; common meaning is not widely established in English
examples: Biriba is a tropical fruit grown in some regions.
Some dictionaries may list biriba under exotic fruits.

senses.definition: not a standard English term; possibly a misspelling or extremely rare usage; no established senses
alternate spelling of biriyani/biryani, a spiced rice dish with meat or vegetables
examples: We had biriyani for lunch.
Biriyani is a famous dish from the region.

senses.definition: plural of biriyani; multiple servings of the spiced rice dish
examples: Different biriyanis were prepared for the party.
She tried several biriyanis from different cooks.

senses.definition: a birk tree or a hill in some dialects; or a surname/place name; also a term in golf for a bunkering term in some locales
examples: The birk tree grows near the old church.
He took a birk on the golf course.

senses.definition: a surname or place-name origin; also a brand name (Birkenstock under German roots) in some contexts
examples: Birken is a common surname in some regions.
Birken stock brand is popular for sandals.

senses.definition: a town in England, also used in naval and ship-naming contexts (Birkenhead is known for Mersey ferry connections)
examples: Birkenhead is across the River Mersey from Liverpool.
The ship was built at Birkenhead docks.

senses.definition: a taxonomic or historical term; may refer to a genus or region; extremely uncommon in contemporary usage
the family name Birkeniidae in paleontology; extremely specialized
a colloquial term for a person named Birke or for The American Birkebeiner cross-country ski race; used regionally
examples: The birkie cheered at the finish line.
Many birkies travel to Wisconsin for the race.
